steps.title: Scale equation 1 by 5
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 5 to match the coefficients in equation 2:
$$5(x + 6y) = 5(-14)$$
$$5x + 30y = -70$$

steps.title: Identify the contradiction
steps.content:
Equation 2 states $5x + 30y = 0$. But we just derived $5x + 30y = -70$ from equation 1. The same expression $5x + 30y$ cannot simultaneously equal both $-70$ and \$0$—this is impossible. Equations 1 and 2 have identical left sides but different right sides, so they are parallel lines that never intersect. Since any valid solution must satisfy all three equations, and equations 1 and 2 already have no common point, the entire system has no solution.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
